job.title: Partnership Services Analyst
job.html_description:
Partnership Services Analyst

Permanent, Full Time

Newcastle upon Tyne
Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger. Build global partnerships that fuel our ambition.

We are the heartbeat of the city. A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast paced and exciting environment.

About the role

We’re looking for a proactive and analytical Partnership Services Analyst to join our Partnerships team. In this role, you’ll use data, insight and market analysis to help shape the partnership strategy across the club. You’ll support both Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing by delivering high‑quality insights, building robust reporting frameworks and ensuring our teams have access to information that drives strong decision‑making.

You’ll play a key role in strengthening our understanding of categories, brands, audiences and performance—helping us build meaningful, evidence‑based partnerships.

What you’ll do

*                 Work collaboratively with the Partnership Services Executive to provide a first‑class service to our Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing teams.

*                 Create data‑driven approaches to identifying key categories and brands for outreach, securing accurate contact details for potential partners.

*                 Provide regular market insight and trend analysis to position the team as a trusted, knowledgeable resource across the club.

*                 Use strong data and insights skills to support the development of compelling sales proposals and outreach materials.

*                 Work with digital colleagues to build systems for analysing digital and data metrics.

*                 Develop reporting mechanisms that demonstrate ROI for existing partners and support the sales team in articulating value to prospects.

The team

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.

What you’ll bring

*                 Strong analytical ability with experience gathering, interpreting and presenting data and insights.

*                 Calm and resilient mindset, especially in a fast‑paced environment.

*                 Good commercial awareness.

*                 Ability to plan and prioritise your workload effectively.

*                 A collaborative and curious approach, with the desire to generate fresh ideas.

*                 Ability to build strong networks across diverse stakeholder groups at the club.

*                 A degree or equivalent industry‑standard experience.

*                 Experience within sport and/or sponsorship sectors would be desirable.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive. We place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply 

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate. 

Right to work

This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.
job.plain_text_description:
Partnership Services Analyst
Permanent, Full Time
Newcastle upon Tyne Competitive Salary
Be part of something bigger. Build global partnerships that fuel our ambition.
We are the heartbeat of the city. A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.
This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast paced and exciting environment.
About the role
We’re looking for a proactive and analytical Partnership Services Analyst to join our Partnerships team. In this role, you’ll use data, insight and market analysis to help shape the partnership strategy across the club. You’ll support both Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing by delivering high‑quality insights, building robust reporting frameworks and ensuring our teams have access to information that drives strong decision‑making.
You’ll play a key role in strengthening our understanding of categories, brands, audiences and performance—helping us build meaningful, evidence‑based partnerships.
What you’ll do
* Work collaboratively with the Partnership Services Executive to provide a first‑class service to our Partnership Development and Partnership Marketing teams.
* Create data‑driven approaches to identifying key categories and brands for outreach, securing accurate contact details for potential partners.
* Provide regular market insight and trend analysis to position the team as a trusted, knowledgeable resource across the club.
* Use strong data and insights skills to support the development of compelling sales proposals and outreach materials.
* Work with digital colleagues to build systems for analysing digital and data metrics.
* Develop reporting mechanisms that demonstrate ROI for existing partners and support the sales team in articulating value to prospects.
The team
You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.
What you’ll bring
* Strong analytical ability with experience gathering, interpreting and presenting data and insights.
* Calm and resilient mindset, especially in a fast‑paced environment.
* Good commercial awareness.
* Ability to plan and prioritise your workload effectively.
* A collaborative and curious approach, with the desire to generate fresh ideas.
* Ability to build strong networks across diverse stakeholder groups at the club.
* A degree or equivalent industry‑standard experience.
* Experience within sport and/or sponsorship sectors would be desirable.
United As One
We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.
Safeguarding
We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive. We place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.
How to apply
To access the full job description for this role please click here.
For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.
Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.
Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.
Right to work
This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

job.title: Senior Academy Scout
job.html_description:
Senior Academy Scout

Permanent 

Competitive Salary
Closing Date: 16th March 2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing people and communities together, join the Newcastle United family as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for a Senior Academy Scout to join our Academy Recruitment team, where you will operate within the Club’s established framework, player profiles and scouting methodology to support informed and effective decision‑making across Talent Identification. You’ll work closely with the Academy Recruitment Director, Academy Scouting Director and Scout Analysts to identify academy‑level players across the UK and Europe, while managing three Regional Scouts across the British Isles.

This is a pivotal role in a highly competitive recruitment environment, requiring deep understanding of development pathways, player traits, scouting insights and emerging talent trends.

Your impact

In this role, you will:

*         Lead the identification and monitoring of academy‑level players across the UK and Europe.

*         Manage Regional Scouts (NI, ROI & Scotland) and ensure structured, high‑quality reporting and six‑weekly cross‑checks.

*         Provide detailed Talent ID insights, maintain accurate coverage records, and support decision‑ready processes for high potential academy players who fit the Newcastle United players profiles.

*         Produce high‑level reports for senior Academy recruitment leaders, outlining opportunities and key emerging players.

*         Influence decision‑making as part of the core recruitment group, ensuring we remain ahead of competitors.

*         Support analysts with pitching videos and contribute to structured workflows and data‑driven insights.

*         Travel internationally as required to assess target players

You’ll play an active leadership role, ensuring CPD is in place for your team and ensuring compliance with people processes and safeguarding expectations.

What you’ll bring

*         Significant experience within a high‑end Premier League recruitment environment.

*         Detailed football knowledge including game models, playing styles and technical/tactical/physical/psychological player traits.

*         Strong discipline in following workflows and excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

*         Experience of key industry systems (e.g., Wyscout, Hudl, Sportscode, Studio).

*         Ability to work independently and remotely while maintaining team cohesion.

*         Strong attention to detail and the ability to make well‑substantiated recommendations.

*         Understanding of how data contributes to recruitment decisions.

*         Growth mindset, adaptability and comfort with evolving recruitment trends.

*         Ability to meet strict deadlines with professionalism and composure.

*         FA Level 3 Talent ID (or equivalent).

Why choose us?

We offer a fantastic range of rewards including, participation in our non‑contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, access to the Helping Hand wellbeing platform, pension contributions, free lunch and refreshments, plus salary‑sacrifice benefits including tech, car and cycle‑to‑work schemes.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self.

Safeguarding

We are dedicated to providing a safe environment where everyone is heard, valued and able to thrive. This role requires an Enhanced DBS with Barred List Check. Safeguarding is everyone’s responsibility, and all colleagues are expected to operate in line with Club policies and best practice.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: Lead Recruitment Analyst
job.html_description:
Lead Recruitment Analyst

Newcastle United Women

Permanent

Newcastle Upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Closing date:-14/03/2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Womens Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

As Lead Recruitment Analyst, you’ll drive our player recruitment strategy by linking live and video scouting with robust data insights. You will manage the operational and analytical engine of the department—curating evidence, creating clarity, and delivering recommendations that align with the vision of the Head of Recruitment & Analysis and the Director of Women’s Football.

Key Responsibilities

  • Player Identification & Scouting
  • Conduct video and live scouting across senior and emerging markets.
  • Produce detailed scouting reports and presentations aligned with club philosophy.
  • Build and manage positional scouting lists.
  • Perform due diligence and background checks on potential recruits.
  • Data & Performance Analysis
  • Use Sportscode, telestration, and video tools to create clips and evidence.
  • Integrate performance data into scouting reports.
  • Maintain a video and data library of target players.
  • Market Intelligence & Benchmarking
  • Research salaries, bonuses, and transfer trends for negotiations.
  • Monitor domestic and international markets for opportunities.
  • Operations & Process Management
  • Manage/ information flow across the scouting network.

Skills & Experience

  • Significant experience in professional football recruitment/scouting.
  • Expertise in video analysis platforms (Sportscode, telestration).
  • Strong data literacy and ability to translate insights into recommendations.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Organised, detail-oriented, and collaborative.
  • Flexibility for travel and live match attendance.
  • Background: Substantial experience in professional football recruitment, analysis or scouting.
  • Technical craft: Strong expertise with video analysis platforms (e.g., Sportscode, telestration) and a high level of data literacy—able to translate insights into clear recommendations.
  • Communication: Excellent written and verbal skills, with the ability to produce concise reports and impactful presentations.
  • Ways of working: Organised, collaborative, and detail‑driven; flexible to attend live matches and travel domestically/internationally as required.

Qualifications

Essential

Degree in Sports Science, Performance Analysis or related field.

FA Talent ID Level 2.

Desirable

Master’s degree in related field.

FA Talent ID Level 3.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, from flexible ways of working, participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discount at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand – where you can access free GP appointments, Wellbeing Resources, Legal and Financial Support, pension contribution, free lunch and the best part, free tea and coffee. In addition, we run a salary sacrifice scheme which includes, tech, car, cycle to work and many more.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please Click here

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: CRM Manager
job.html_description:
CRM Manager

Permanent, Full Time

Newcastle upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger. Turn data into smart decisions - personalising every experience at scale. 

We are the heartbeat of the city. A Club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast paced and exciting environment.

About the role

We’re looking for a CRM Manager to play a key role in delivering the Club’s global CRM strategy focused on fan acquisition, engagement and lifetime value. Working alongside the CRM lead, you will take responsibility for translating strategic objectives into actionable plans and ensuring CRM activity is delivered to a high standard across all stages of the fan lifecycle.

You will own the end-to-end delivery of CRM campaigns from briefing through to audience creation, execution and performance analysis. You will also lead the development and optimisation of automated lifecycle journeys that nurture fans throughout their relationship with the Club. As a central point of contact for stakeholders, you will ensure CRM activity aligns with business priorities and enhances the fan experience.

Hands on experience with ESPs and CDPs is essential. Bloomreach experience is ideal, but Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze or similar platforms are also valuable.

What you’ll do

*        Help shape and deliver the Club’s CRM strategy focused on fan acquisition, engagement and commercial performance.

*        Translate strategic objectives in a multichannel CRM plan, ensuring alignment across key stakeholders and teams.

*        Own the end-to-end delivery of CRM campaigns including briefing, audience segmentation, build, QA, execution and analysis.

*        Lead the creation, implementation and optimisation of automated lifecycle journeys that drive onboarding, engagement, retention and reactivation.

*        Maintain high standards of data governance ensuring compliance with GDPR and UK data protection requirements.

*        Drive data capture initiatives across all touchpoints, working cross‑functionally to track KPIs and test new ways to improve acquisition.

*        Use insights and reporting to optimise CRM performance and improve fan experience.

*        Manage CRM database hygiene practices and integration of new data sources

About you

*        Demonstrable experience working in CRM, lifecycle marketing, with a strong track record of delivery effective campaigns.

*        Comfortable taking ownership of projects and leading CRM activity from planning through to execution and review.

*        Confident working with customer data and building audiences using CRM tools such as Bloomreach, Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Braze (Bloomreach preferred but not essential).

*        Strong understanding of how to create and improve lifecycle strategies that drive conversion, engagement and retention.

*        Highly organised with the ability to manage several projects at once and adapt to changing priorities.

*        Skilled at working with different teams and building strong relationships with stakeholders at all levels.

*        Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a high attention to detail.

*        Strong understanding of GDPR and UK data protection requirements.

*        Experience managing or developing team members.

The team

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, collaboration and a positive mindset. You’ll work closely with colleagues across Marketing, Digital, Retail and Analytics, as well as external partners.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply  

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club please visit our careers page. 

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates. 

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate. 

Right to work

This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All offers are subject to background checks, including references, DBS where applicable, and verification of eligibility to work. Please note we are unable to sponsor applicants without existing right‑to‑work status.

job.title: Academy Scouting Operations Manager
job.html_description:
Job Title: Academy Scouting Operations Manager

Location: Remote

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: Competitive

Closing Date: 22nd March 2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for a highly organised and strategically minded Academy Scouting Operations Manager to lead the operational planning, coordination, and delivery of all scouting activity across the Academy’s European scouting arm. In this role, you will work within the MCO’s established framework and methodology to drive scouting workflows, manage key administrative processes, and ensure the accurate assessment and prioritisation of scouting data.

Your impact

In this role, you will oversee the operational engine behind our European academy scouting network. You will organise scouting workflows, create weekly and monthly schedules, and act as the key point of contact for scout requests. You’ll maintain schedule planners, assign fixtures and reports, manage databases and squad lists, and ensure all scouting information is captured accurately and efficiently.

About the team

You will work within the Academy Recruitment Department, collaborating closely with the Academy Recruitment Director, Academy Scouting Director, international regional scouts, and the UK scouting team.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, including participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts, wellbeing support, pension contribution, free lunch and refreshments, and salary sacrifice schemes.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all.

Safeguarding

This role requires an enhanced criminal records check (BLC) due to responsibilities relating to young people and adults at risk.

How to apply

To view the full job description for this role, please click here.

Please apply via our careers page. Early applications are encouraged as the vacancy may close early.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.
